title: chamaecrista zygophylloides var. colligans
intro: Chamaecrista zygophylloides var. colligans is a plant species commonly known as the "stinging spurge," a member of the Fabaceae family. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to have medicinal properties in treating various health conditions. It is reported to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions, among others. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for consumption, and its roots and leaves can also be used to make salves and creams for topical application.
